channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S. military launches new Iran strikes as Trump says 'not sure' he wants deal
item.description: Centcom said it launched dozens of strikes on Iranian military infrastructure and small boats; Trump said the U.S. might take more military action "tonight."

item.title: SpaceX stock closes below debut price at $148 in two-day slide after Nasdaq-100 inclusion
item.description: SpaceX's record IPO raised a total of $85.7 billion after underwriters exercised the "greenshoe" overallotment.

item.title: McConnell health update demanded by Gov. Beshear as Senate vacancy questions grow
item.description: McConnell, the former longtime Senate leader who declined to seek an eighth term following a series of health scares, was admitted to a hospital on June 14.

item.title: Fed officials were split on direction of interest rates at last meeting, minutes show
item.description: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released minutes from its June 16-17 meeting.

item.title: SpaceX is racing toward orbital data centers. Here are ways to play this emerging technology
item.description: SpaceX’s push toward orbital data centers opens new avenues for AI infrastructure. Wall Street is already mapping the stocks that could benefit.

item.title: Dow slides more than 570 points as oil spikes and Trump threatens another attack on Iran: Live updates
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump told the NATO summit in Turkey that the ceasefire with Iran is "over."

item.title: Judge orders E. Jean Carroll be paid $5 million in damages from Trump verdict
item.description: Trump was held civilly liable in two trials for defaming E. Jean Carroll when he denied her claim he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

item.title: Levi Strauss beats quarterly expectations, raises guidance and dividend
item.description: Levi Strauss beat quarterly expectations on the top and bottom lines during its fiscal 2026 second quarter.

item.title: Michael Burry bets on sportsbooks DraftKings and Flutter, sees prediction markets curbed by regulation
item.description: The investor believes regulators will eventually crack down on prediction markets after competition from the upstarts pressured the stocks of sportsbooks.

item.title: Meta is building its first big Canadian data center as AI expansion crosses the border
item.description: Meta is building its first big Canadian data center as AI expansion crosses the border.

item.title: Trump says he doesn't want anything to do with Spain: 'Cut off all trade'
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump slammed Spain for not contributing enough to NATO, as he attended the defense alliance summit in Turkey.

item.title: Trump pours cold water on NATO allies' united front
item.description: Trump criticized NATO allies over defense spending, attacked Spain and renewed his Greenland push during the alliance’s summit in Turkey.

item.title: Delta launches 'basic business' fares without lounge access, seat selection
item.description: Delta is launching basic business and other stripped-down premium classes that come with fewer perks.

item.title: Where this top bond manager is investing for income in the second half
item.description: As the bond landscape shifts, Franklin Templeton Fixed Income chief investment officer Sonal Desai is finding ample opportunities for income.

item.title: Oil prices jump more than 4% after Trump threatens to bomb Iran and reimpose naval blockade
item.description: Speaking at the NATO summit in Turkey, Trump said he considered the ceasefire with Iran over. His comments came after U.S. airstrikes overnight.

item.title: Fed meeting minutes to show 'family fight' over rates. The squabble could drag on for a while
item.description: There have been few instances over the past 35 years or so when the Fed has only made one rate move, be it up or down.

item.title: Rivian tailspin hasn't shaken one trader's resolve
item.description: Mike Khouw revisits his Rivian trade after the stock tanks on a share offering.

item.title: Trump loses latest appeals court bid to restore his name to Kennedy Center
item.description: The appeals court said Trump had failed to provide any evidence that the Kennedy Center's fundraising would be harmed if his name is not attached.

item.title: Waymo to start driverless rides in 4 more U.S. markets as expansion accelerates
item.description: Waymo has a big lead in the nascent U.S. robotaxi market, and is opening up in more cities.

item.title: Used EVs keep getting more expensive amid Iran war, high gas prices
item.description: The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index for EVs — which tracks prices of used vehicles sold at its U.S. wholesale auctions — increased 12% compared to June 2025.

item.title: Few bright spots in Wednesday's tough market — why Nvidia stock is one of them
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Private student loans with rewards: These lenders offer cash back and discounts
item.description: Find out where student loan borrowers can earn cash back for getting good grades, graduating, paying on time and more.

item.title: Delaying Social Security reform raises risks for bond markets and the economy, research finds
item.description: Social Security's trust fund that helps pay retirement benefits is projected to run out in late 2032. New research finds that may pose serious economic risks.

item.title: The American Dream is missing one key ingredient, says Harvard happiness expert: Without it, 'life is pretty grim'
item.description: Only 35% of U.S. adults define the American Dream as being part of a community. But one cannot thrive without the people around them, say experts.

item.title: Bezos' Blue Origin valued at $130 billion in first outside fundraising round
item.description: Bezos' Blue Origin is raising outside capital in a round valuing the rocket company at $130 billion, sources told CNBC's Andrew Ross Sorkin.

item.title: Apple commits $30 billion to Broadcom for U.S. chipmaking push
item.description: Apple is expanding its Broadcom partnership in a $30 billion-plus chipmaking agreement, its largest American manufacturing commitment to date.

item.title: Mitch McConnell's health concerns grow amid 4th week in hospital with few details from aides
item.description: McConnell, the former longtime Senate leader who declined to seek an eighth term following a series of health scares, was admitted to the hospital on June 14.

item.title: Healthy Returns: Employers aren't expanding coverage of GLP-1 obesity drugs — many are finding ways around it
item.description: About 36% of employers said they provide coverage of GLP-1s for both diabetes and weight loss, the same percentage as 2025 and up from 34% in 2024, the survey said.

item.title: OpenAI to publicly release GPT-5.6, rolls out conversational AI models
item.description: OpenAI's chief rival, Anthropic, recently restored access to its latest models following a weeks-long clash with the government.

item.title: Manhattan office leasing sees strongest gains in 20 years
item.description: During the second quarter, office leasing volumes were 31.3% above the 10-year average, according to a new report from Colliers.
